Chat with Tafseer

Purpose
- Provide verse-scoped tafseer explanations and chapter-level tafseer retrieval.
- Use Quran.com as the upstream tafseer source.
- Use Ollama only to answer from supplied tafseer context.

Available endpoints
1. POST /chat
   - Required JSON fields: resource_id (int), verse_key (string chapter:verse), message (string)
   - Optional JSON field: thread_id (string)
   - Returns: answer, resource_id, verse_key, chapter_number
   - Use this when an end user asks a question about a specific verse and you already know the verse.

2. GET /tafseer/{resource_id}/chapter/{chapter_number}
   - Returns raw chapter tafseer data from Quran.com.
   - Use this when you need source tafseer content by chapter without chat or model generation.

3. GET /surahs
   - Returns a list of all 114 Surahs (chapters) with their metadata (number, names, ayah count).
   - Use this to populate a Surah selection menu or index.

How to use /chat correctly
- Resolve the verse in your client or workflow before calling the API.
- Do not ask the backend to guess the verse from free-form user prose.
- Reuse the same thread_id only for follow-up questions on the same verse.
- If the verse changes, use a different thread_id.

Thread behavior
- Same thread_id + same verse keeps recent conversation history.
- Same thread_id + different verse resets cached context to the new verse.

Error behavior
- 404: tafseer resource or verse not found upstream
- 422: invalid request body
- 502: upstream Quran API failure or generic model failure
- 503: Ollama unavailable or configured model missing

Current scope
- No authentication
- No free-form surah parsing
- No published frontend SDK package
